rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (4/10) and cite the same core concerns — lack of direct experience in AI accelerators, SoC and IP modeling, and performance analysis for deep-learning workloads. The strengths and justifications differ only in wording (e.g., 'Diverse programming experience' vs 'Experience with diverse technologies'), which is ordinary run-to-run paraphrasing. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change to Mohammed Al-Said.
